title: Cephaelis chiapensis
intro: Cephaelis chiapensis, also known as Chiapas coffee tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis chiapensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried root and bark extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Cephaelis chiapensis belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Rubioideae, which contains the genus Cephaelis. The genus Cephaelis itself is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Cephaelis chiapensis is a specific species within this genus and is characterized by its unique leaf and flower morphology. The classification of Cephaelis chiapensis reflects its evolutionary relationships and position within the Rubiaceae family.
